candidates.title: Activation Energy Theory - Routing Failure Analysis

collisions.paper: A Probabilistic Inference Scaling Theory for LLM Self-Correction
collisions.overlap: EXACT MATCH: The proposal's formula P_k(correct) = P_inf * (1 - exp(-k/k_0)) is mathematically equivalent to the paper's Acc_t = Upp - alpha^t(Upp - Acc_0). Both describe exponential convergence/saturation.
collisions.differentiation: Yang et al. uses CL (confidence level) and CS (critique score). The proposal uses Ea (activation energy) = P_inf * k_0. The underlying math is the same exponential form.

collisions.paper: Decomposing LLM Self-Correction: The Accuracy-Correction Paradox and Error Depth Hypothesis
collisions.overlap: Error Depth Hypothesis supports H4: calculation errors 62% (GPT-3.5), 22% (DeepSeek). 'Shallow' errors = calculation mistakes are easier to self-correct.
collisions.differentiation: Li frames via error depth and self-correction. Proposal frames via Ea-based routing failure mechanism.

collisions.paper: ACAR: Adaptive Complexity Routing for Multi-Model Ensembles with Auditable Decision Traces
collisions.overlap: 'Agreement-but-wrong' ceiling (8pp gap) confirms H3 falsification. Entropy routing shows weak correlation with ground truth - directly challenges H5.
collisions.differentiation: ACAR uses sigma-based routing. Proposal uses Ea-based routing. Both find consistency methods have fundamental limits.

candidates.differentiation_notes: H3 negative result (Ea does NOT predict single-pass threshold) is the core novel contribution. H4 aligns with Li's Error Depth Hypothesis. H5 at HIGH RISK due to ACAR finding entropy ineffective as routing signal.

H1_Arrhenius.reason: Identical to Yang et al. exponential saturation formula

H2_Ea_Difficulty.reason: Related to Yang et al. CL-CS model

H3_Single_Pass_Threshold.reason: First systematic falsification on MATH/Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct. ACAR confirms 'agreement-but-wrong' ceiling.

H4_Execution_Errors.reason: Novel in routing context. Li (2601.00828) supports calculation error dominance (62%).

H5_Entropy_Routing.reason: ACAR (2602.21231) explicitly found 'entropy showed weak correlation' with ground truth. HIGH RISK of failure.

key_concerns: Yang et al. 2025 (2508.16456) collision: exponential formula not novel
H1 and H2 are replications of Yang et al.'s work
H5 at HIGH RISK due to ACAR finding entropy routing ineffective
genuinely_novel_claims: Negative result: Ea-based routing does NOT predict single-pass solveability (H3)
Diagnostic analysis: execution errors vs conceptual errors explain routing failure (H4)
Comparison of Ea vs entropy as routing signals (H5, at risk)

summary: Proposal pivots to H3 negative result (Ea does NOT predict single-pass threshold) with H4 diagnostic analysis. H5 at HIGH RISK due to ACAR finding entropy routing ineffective. Li (2601.00828) supports H4. Yang et al. collision remains for H1-H2.
critical_papers_found: Yang et al. 2025 (2508.16456) - EXACT COLLISION on exponential saturation formula
Li 2026 (2601.00828) - Error Depth Hypothesis SUPPORTS H4 (calculation errors 62%)
ACAR 2026 (2602.21231) - AGREEMENT-BUT-WRONG confirms H3; entropy weak challenges H5

risk_flags.risk: Yang et al. 2025 collision: H1-H2 cannot claim novelty
H5 at HIGH RISK: ACAR found entropy routing shows weak correlation
Novelty claim must focus on H3 negative result + H4 diagnostic

recommendations.H3: PROCEED - Reposition as first systematic falsification of Ea-based routing on MATH benchmark. Acknowledge ACAR's related 'agreement-but-wrong' finding.
recommendations.H4: PROCEED - Novel application in routing context. Align with Li's Error Depth Hypothesis but focus on routing failure mechanism.
recommendations.H5: HIGH RISK - ACAR explicitly found entropy routing ineffective. Consider dropping or making exploratory only.
recommendations.overall: Focus on H3 (negative result) + H4 (diagnostic). Drop or de-emphasize H5.

evidence_sources: Yang et al. 2025 (arXiv:2508.16456) - 'A Probabilistic Inference Scaling Theory for LLM Self-Correction'
Li 2026 (arXiv:2601.00828) - 'Decomposing LLM Self-Correction: The Accuracy-Correction Paradox and Error Depth Hypothesis'
ACAR 2026 (arXiv:2602.21231) - 'ACAR: Adaptive Complexity Routing for Multi-Model Ensembles'
Round 4 pilot results: H1 (R²=0.936), H2 (Spearman=0.578), H3 (68.4% < 75%)
